summary: Matched the remaining common fighter run/dash physics routines in src/melee/ft/ft_0C88.c, adding implementations for ftCo_Dash_Phys, ftCo_Run_Phys, ftCo_RunDirect_Phys, and ftCo_RunBrake_Phys. The PR also refined shared motion-variable layout for dash/run in ftCommon_MotionVars and typed/renamed ftCommonData fields used by the acceleration/friction logic. The decomp.dev report confirmed 4 new matches totaling +460 bytes.
what_changed: Added a static inline helper getAccelAndTarget(Fighter* fp, float* accel, float* target_vel) to compute dash/run acceleration and terminal target velocity from input.lstick.x and ftCo_DatAttrs fields dash_run_acceleration_a, dash_run_acceleration_b, and dash_run_terminal_velocity.

Updated ftCo_TurnRun_Phys to use getAccelAndTarget and the target_vel naming instead of local max_vel calculation; its friction multiplier use changed from p_ftCommonData->x60_turnrunFrictionMul to p_ftCommonData->x60_someFrictionMul.

Implemented ftCo_Dash_Phys. It checks fp->mv.co.dash.x0 as a one-frame flag, clears it when set, otherwise computes accel/target_vel and calls ftCommon_8007C98C with ground friction scaled by p_ftCommonData->x60_someFrictionMul, then calls ftCommon_8007CB74.

Implemented ftCo_Run_Phys. It computes accel/target_vel, scales acceleration when current ground velocity is between 0 and target velocity, stores target_vel * p_ftCommonData->x440 into fp->mv.co.run.x4, applies ftCommon_8007C98C, and then calls ftCommon_8007CB74.
Implemented ftCo_RunDirect_Phys as a non-inlined wrapper around ftCo_Run_Phys using #pragma push, #pragma dont_inline on, and #pragma pop.
Implemented ftCo_RunBrake_Phys as friction-only ground deceleration via ftCommon_8007C930(fp, p_ftCommonData->x60_someFrictionMul * fp->co_attrs.gr_friction), followed by ftCommon_8007CB74.
Added ftCommon_MotionVars union members for dash and run: dash.x0 at fp+2340, and run.x4 at fp+2344 with padding.
Changed ftCommonData field +5C from UNK_T x5C to float x5C, and renamed/retained +60 as float x60_someFrictionMul instead of x60_turnrunFrictionMul.
smart_moves: Factored the repeated dash/run acceleration and target velocity formula into getAccelAndTarget, reducing duplication across TurnRun, Dash, and Run physics while preserving matching behavior.
Used pointer out-parameters in the static inline helper, a common decomp tactic when the original compiler's register/stack behavior needs to be shaped without introducing a standalone symbol.
Kept ftCo_RunDirect_Phys as a tiny wrapper around ftCo_Run_Phys but explicitly prevented inlining, matching the separate 32-byte function reported by decomp.dev.
Added precise motion-variable union members only where the physics implementations needed them, preserving offsets with padding rather than over-expanding the struct.
Improved type information for ftCommonData +5C from unknown to float because ftCo_Run_Phys multiplies it into a floating-point acceleration scale.
decomp_lessons: When several related physics states share the same acceleration formula, a static inline helper can make the C clearer and still match if its call pattern and out-parameter behavior align with the original codegen.
MotionVars union additions should be anchored to observed fp offsets. Here dash.x0 maps to fp+2340 and run.x4 maps to fp+2344 with explicit padding.
A function that appears behaviorally identical to another state handler may still need to exist as a separate non-inlined wrapper to match the original binary's function boundaries.

Renaming a common-data field too narrowly can become misleading as more call sites are matched. The +0x60 multiplier was broadened from turnrun-specific x60_turnrunFrictionMul to x60_someFrictionMul after it was used by Dash, Run, RunBrake, and TurnRun physics.
Bot match reports are useful evidence for scope: this PR moved ftCo_Run_Phys, ftCo_Dash_Phys, ftCo_RunBrake_Phys, and ftCo_RunDirect_Phys from 0% to 100% in main/melee/ft/ft_0C88.
naming_conventions: Helper name used lower camel case: getAccelAndTarget.
Local physics variables use descriptive float names accel and target_vel instead of less precise max_vel.
Common data unknowns remain offset-based when semantics are incomplete, e.g. x5C and x440; a slightly semantic but still cautious name was used for x60_someFrictionMul.
ftCommon_MotionVars state-specific fields are nested under state names matching action state terminology: mv.co.dash.x0, mv.co.run.x4, mv.co.turnrun.accel_mul.
Matched common fighter action-state functions keep the ftCo_<State>_<Callback> pattern, such as ftCo_Dash_Phys, ftCo_Run_Phys, ftCo_RunDirect_Phys, and ftCo_RunBrake_Phys.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(1) in ftCo_Dash_Phys, indicating stack-shape padding was needed for matching.
Used #pragma dont_inline around ftCo_RunDirect_Phys to force a separate wrapper function rather than allowing the compiler to inline ftCo_Run_Phys.
Introduced a static inline helper rather than a normal helper symbol, likely to share source logic while avoiding an emitted function.
Preserved branch-sensitive acceleration clamping in ftCo_TurnRun_Phys and sign-sensitive terminal-velocity comparisons using target_vel.
Used float typing for ftCommonData x5C so arithmetic in ftCo_Run_Phys emits floating-point operations.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

decomp-dev[bot] reported GALE01 matched code increased to 27.64% (+0.01%, +460 bytes) with 4 new matches: ftCo_Run_Phys (+208), ftCo_Dash_Phys (+148), ftCo_RunBrake_Phys (+72), and ftCo_RunDirect_Phys (+32), all from 0.00% to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftCommonData x5C, x60_someFrictionMul, or x440 with stronger semantic names.
Search for other ftCommon_MotionVars dash/run fields to confirm whether dash.x0 and run.x4 were later documented.
Look for subsequent matches of ftCo_Dash_IASA, ftCo_Run_IASA, ftCo_RunDirect_IASA, or related collision callbacks in ft_0C88.c.
Search for other uses of getAccelAndTarget or equivalent dash_run_acceleration_a/b formulas in fighter movement code.
Compare future PRs using #pragma dont_inline wrappers for action-state aliases to reuse the RunDirect matching tactic.
